etymology.description: 䰜 (cauldron) + 米 (rice) = congee cooked in a cauldron -> 鬻 (congee)
etymology.interpretation: The character 鬻 (yù, 'congee') represents the cooking of rice in a cauldron, depicted by the components 䰜 (cauldron) and 米 (rice), with the dual 弓 (depicting steam) illustrating the process of boiling. Though originally meaning 'congee,' 鬻 was later borrowed to represent 'selling,' separating it from 粥 (zhōu, 'congee'), a simplified form retaining the original culinary sense.
